title: Spiral ledger record
description: One JSONL line in a spiral ledger. Two files, append-only: changes.jsonl (verified second-order self-changes — the system editing itself) and evals.jsonl (automated measurements that back them). A 'measure-run' summary line may appear in evals.jsonl. The format is the project; tools are replaceable.

change.description: One verified self-change (changes.jsonl). Class is declared, but must be backed by the evidence field — an auditor should be able to follow it.

ts.description: ISO date or datetime of the verified change

class.description: ship = moved a measured delta>0 (or removed a documented failure mode); gate = structural, verified by review/self-test, no single-shot delta; null = honest closure (revert, convergence, correction). A ledger with zero null lines is not measuring.

delta.description: measured percentage-point delta for ship changes; never invented for gate/null

grounding.description: external = triggered by a signal from outside the loop (a borrow, a paper, a user correction, a production outcome); internal = self-generated. The Data Processing Inequality caps what a closed loop can learn.

evidence.description: where the proof lives: an eval record ref, a review verdict, a self-test, a revert commit

target.description: what part of the system was changed

eval.description: One automated with-vs-without measurement (evals.jsonl). Delta and difficulty are MODEL-RELATIVE: records never merge across models.

skill.description: the intervention under test (path or id); null for baseline-only runs

judgeModel.description: grader model — MUST be stronger than `model`; judge==model fabricates results

n.description: runs per case; case verdict is the majority

baseline.description: majority verdict without the intervention; null = all runs errored

graded.description: cases where both arms produced a verdict

errors.description: cases excluded as errored — recorded, never hidden

gate.description: PASS iff skillPass > baselinePass; ERROR recorded honestly when a run dies

tokens.description: real usage when the API reports it; null when unmeasurable — never an invented constant

dir.description: where per-case transcripts were persisted (diagnosis artifacts)

measureRun.description: Summary line appended after measuring every set (evals.jsonl).
